title: RFC 9309 Matching, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Go Adapter · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs rfc 9309 matching, failure semantics, caching, and go adapter from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for conformance-tested robots decision engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin with an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 product-token matching, group merge, percent encoding, longest rule, and allow tie behavior.
Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat sitemap, crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as separately documented hints or owner policy. Artifact under review: conformance-tested robots decision engine.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Robots Extension Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Robots Extension Policy record says conformance-tested robots decision engine looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Robots Extension Policy.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Robots Extension Policy for conformance-tested robots decision engine.
